CustomNPC+ Javadoc Reference
Back to Index
  • All Classes
 

Package noppes.npcs.api.entity

  • Interface Summary 
    Interface Description
    IAnimal<T extends net.minecraft.entity.passive.EntityAnimal>
    Represents an animal entity with additional behavior such as breeding, following, and mating.
    IAnimatable
    Represents an entity capable of playing animations.
    IArrow<T extends net.minecraft.entity.projectile.EntityArrow>
    Represents a arrow entity with additional methods.
    ICustomNpc<T extends net.minecraft.entity.EntityCreature>
    Represents a customizable NPC with a wide variety of properties such as appearance, behavior, combat, loot, and more.
    IDBCPlayer
    Extends IPlayer with additional methods specific to DBC (Dragon Ball C) players.
    IEnergyAbility<T extends net.minecraft.entity.Entity>
    Base interface for all energy ability entities (Projectiles, Barriers).
    IEnergyBarrier<T extends net.minecraft.entity.Entity>
    Base interface for energy barrier entities (Dome, Panel).
    IEnergyBeam<T extends net.minecraft.entity.Entity>
    A directional beam projectile with a trailing path and optional head orb.
    IEnergyDisc<T extends net.minecraft.entity.Entity>
    A flat spinning disc projectile with optional boomerang behavior.
    IEnergyDome<T extends net.minecraft.entity.Entity>
    Represents an energy dome barrier - a spherical shield centered on the caster.
    IEnergyExplosion<T extends net.minecraft.entity.Entity>
    Represents a standalone energy explosion entity.
    IEnergyLaser<T extends net.minecraft.entity.Entity>
    A continuous laser beam that extends outward from its origin along a direction vector.
    IEnergyOrb<T extends net.minecraft.entity.Entity>
    A spherical homing energy ball.
    IEnergyPanel<T extends net.minecraft.entity.Entity>
    Represents an energy panel barrier - a flat rectangular shield (Wall or Shield mode).
    IEnergyProjectile<T extends net.minecraft.entity.Entity>
    Base interface for all energy projectile entities (Orb, Beam, Disc, Laser, Slicer).
    IEnergySlicer<T extends net.minecraft.entity.Entity>
    A thin, wide blade projectile that slices through targets.
    IEnergySweeper<T extends net.minecraft.entity.Entity>
    Entity-level interface for sweeper beam entities.
    IEnergyZone<T extends net.minecraft.entity.Entity>
    Entity-level interface for zone entities (Hazard and Trap).
    IEntity<T extends net.minecraft.entity.Entity>
    Represents a generic entity in the world.
    IEntityItem<T extends net.minecraft.entity.item.EntityItem>
    Represents an item entity in the world.
    IEntityLiving<T extends net.minecraft.entity.EntityLiving>
    Represents a living entity (mob, NPC, etc.) that extends the base living functionality.
    IEntityLivingBase<T extends net.minecraft.entity.EntityLivingBase>
    Represents a living entity (mob, NPC, etc.) with additional methods to manage health, damage, targeting, vision, potion effects, equipment, and various attributes.
    IFishHook<T extends net.minecraft.entity.projectile.EntityFishHook>
    Represents a fish hook entity with additional methods.
    IMonster<T extends net.minecraft.entity.monster.EntityMob>
    Represents a monster entity.
    IPixelmon<T extends net.minecraft.entity.passive.EntityTameable>
    Represents a Pixelmon (a tameable creature) with additional attributes such as shiny state, level, IV/EV values, nature, moves, and more.
    IPlayer<T extends net.minecraft.entity.player.EntityPlayerMP>
    Represents a player in the game with methods for managing dialogs, quests, inventory, sound, and more.
    IProjectile
    Represents a projectile entity.
    IThrowable<T extends net.minecraft.entity.projectile.EntityThrowable>
    Represents a throwable entity with additional methods.
    IVillager<T extends net.minecraft.entity.passive.EntityVillager>
    Represents a villager entity with additional trading-related methods.
